questions.text: Is the dominant background color on the agent's page perceptually similar to the reference's — close enough that a casual viewer would not notice the difference?
Is the primary accent color on the agent's page similar to the reference's primary accent?

Is the secondary accent color on the agent's page similar to the reference's? Treat 'neither render uses a secondary accent' as a perfect match (score 5). If the agent's secondary accent differs noticeably from the reference's, score ≤ 2.
Do both renders read with the same temperature — warm vs. cool vs. neutral?
Is the text/background contrast on the agent's page comparable to the reference's?
Do both renders show a similar level of saturation — neither obviously more vivid or more washed-out than the other?
Is the accent color on the agent's page applied to comparable UI elements as on the reference (primary buttons, active states, links, badges) — same color used for the same kinds of things?

Is the proportion of background color vs. accent color usage similar to the reference — e.g., both mostly white with small accent splashes, or both predominantly dark with bright highlights, or both heavily saturated throughout? Answer 0 if one is overwhelmingly neutral while the other is overwhelmingly colorful.
Has the agent avoided introducing colors that are clearly absent from the reference — no obviously new hues that weren't anywhere in the reference's palette?
